item.title: Simplify AC and step accumulation in backlog add and edit
item.intent: All array-building in backlog add and edit uses shared helpers. The pending_ac flush pattern, manual AC JSON string-cat, and triplicated scope/steps/deps append blocks are eliminated. Saves ~50 lines combined.
item.description: Three patterns need fixing: (1) pending_ac/flush state machine for --ac/--ac-verify pairing — simplify to push-immediately-on-ac, modify-last-on-ac-verify. (2) AC JSON array built by manual string concatenation (10 identical lines in both add and edit) — extract _build_ac_json helper. (3) Three identical scope/steps/deps append blocks in edit (23 lines) each doing the same if-gt-0/printf/jq-R/jq-s/jq_updates pattern — extract _append_array_field helper. Also remove update_desc tracking from edit (14 lines of string concatenation for a cosmetic log message).
steps: Extract _build_ac_json helper that converts ac_entries array to JSON array string
Replace manual AC string-cat in cmd_backlog_add with _build_ac_json call
Replace manual AC string-cat in cmd_backlog_edit with _build_ac_json call
Extract _append_array_field helper: takes field name, bash array, and jq update expression
Replace the 3 scope/steps/deps append blocks in cmd_backlog_edit with _append_array_field calls
Refactor --ac to push immediately; --ac-verify modifies last entry; remove pending_ac
Remove update_desc tracking from cmd_backlog_edit; simplify success message to Updated ID
Verify: add and edit with all flag combinations produce identical JSON output
acceptanceCriteria: No pending_ac variable exists in the script
No manual AC JSON string concatenation (first=true loop pattern) exists in the script
A shared _build_ac_json helper is used by both add and edit
A shared _append_array_field helper replaces the 3 identical blocks in edit
backlog add with --ac and --ac-verify produces correct JSON
backlog edit with --ac, --step, --scope, --depends-on produces correct JSON
